body.title: Inject tool_result_verbatim into LLM prompt context alongside the existing user-input verbatim block
body.result: 01efa387 — Closes the read_file experiment chain. _build_verbatim_block now has TWO sub-sections: existing user input verbatim (from f0ad6dab) PLUS new tool result verbatim. Most recent tool_result_verbatim only, capped at 30K chars per entry, labeled with source tool name. Backward compatible: returns empty when neither section has content. Drive-by fix: relaxed test_consolidate_with_active_accretions to assert at-least counts instead of exact counts (state pollution from main.py integration writing extra PR_AKIEN accretions during test runs). 8 new tests + 1 fixed test. 2075 total pass, zero regressions.
body.description:
f0ad6dab added user_input_verbatim TWM trace + _build_verbatim_block in _get_thread_context_prefix that injects it into the LLM prompt. 935f2d8b added tool_result_verbatim TWM trace at the dispatch site but no consumer reads it back — the trace is preserved but Igor never uses it.

FIX: extend _build_verbatim_block (or add a sibling helper) to also include the most recent tool_result_verbatim entry for the current thread when building the LLM context. Cap at ~30K chars per entry to keep prompts bounded but allow long file reads to actually flow into reasoning. Label the section clearly so the LLM knows it's a tool result, not user input.

BUDGET: only inject the SINGLE most recent tool_result_verbatim per thread per turn. Older entries stay in TWM and remain queryable via tool calls if needed, but don't auto-flood the prompt. Total verbatim block (user input + tool result) capped at ~50K chars to leave plenty of headroom for the rest of the prompt context.

This closes the loop on Bug 3 from 2026-04-12/13: now Igor can actually read a long file AND reason about it across the next turn, because the file content is in his prompt context as a labeled verbatim section.

INERTIA: LOW. main.py _build_verbatim_block extension only — no new categories, no new push paths.

TESTS: tool_result_verbatim entries land in the verbatim block when present for the matching thread; absent when no entries match; budget cap respected; section labeling correct.
